name: Marinated Peppers
recipeInstructions:
Yield: About 9 half-pints.
(Bell, Hungarian, Banana, or Jalapeno)
Note: You can adjust the intensity of pickled jalapeno peppers by using all hot jalapeno peppers (hot style), or blending with sweet and mild peppers (medium or mild style).
For hot style: Use 4 pounds jalapeno peppers.
For medium style: Use 2 pounds jalapeno peppers and 2 pounds sweet and mild peppers.
For mild style: Use 1 pound jalapeno peppers and 3 pounds sweet and mild peppers.
Procedure:
Select your favorite pepper. Caution: If you select hot peppers, wear rubber or plastic gloves while handling them or wash hands thoroughly with soap and water before touching your face.
Peppers may be left whole. Large peppers may be quartered. Wash, slash two to four slits in each pepper, and blanch in boiling water or blister on a hot surface until the skins start to char.
Peppers may be blistered using one of the following methods: Oven/broiler method: Place peppers in a hot oven (400°F) or broiler for 6-8 minutes or until skins blister. Range-top method: Cover a hot burner with heavy wire mesh; place peppers on the burner for several minutes until skins blister.
Allow peppers to cool. Place in a pan and cover with a damp cloth. This will make peeling the peppers easier. After several minutes of cooling, peel each pepper. Flatten whole peppers.
Mix all remaining ingredients in a saucepan and heat to boiling. Add 1/4 garlic clove (optional) and 1/4 teaspoon salt per half-pint or 1/2 teaspoon per pint. Fill jars with peppers, add hot, well-mixed oil/pickling solution over peppers, leaving 1/2-inch headspace.
Adjust lids and process according to the recommendations in Table 1.
Table 1. Recommended processing time for Marinated Peppers in a boiling-water canner.
Style of Pack: Raw. Jar Size: Half-pints and Pints.
Process Time at Altitudes of 0 - 1,000 ft: 15 minutes
1,001 - 3,000 ft: 20 minutes
3,001 - 6,000 ft: 20 minutes
Above 6,000 ft: 25 minutes
